title: Human Development Index groups
subtitle: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.
caption:
Human Development Index groups, 2023

The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.

This choropleth map displays the global distribution of countries across four Human Development Index groups for the year 2023, categorized by color intensity from red (Low) to dark blue (Very high). The "Very high" development group, represented by dark blue, encompasses the majority of North America, including the United States and Canada, as well as most of Western and Northern Europe, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, and South Korea. A lighter blue shade indicates the "High" group, which covers much of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, alongside Russia, China, and several nations in Eastern Europe and the Middle East. The "Medium" group, shown in yellow, includes countries such as India, Indonesia, Mexico, and a band of nations across North Africa and parts of Southeast Asia. The "Low" group, colored in red, is concentrated primarily in Sub-Saharan Africa, covering a large contiguous area from the Sahel down to the southern coast, as well as Yemen and Afghanistan. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, indicating that no data is available for this region. The visual gradient reveals a distinct geographic clustering, with the highest development levels concentrated in the Northern Hemisphere's temperate zones and Oceania, while the lowest levels are predominantly located in central and western Africa.
